ARX Türkiye Newsletter

April 2026
Team Spirit Team Growth Business Development Latest News

Dear ARX Türkiye Team,

This newsletter has been created to highlight the projects we deliver, the achievements we accomplish, and the value we generate together at ARX Türkiye. It provides a snapshot of our teams' contributions, key developments, and ongoing progress.

Over the past year, we have achieved approximately 170% team growth, reflecting the strength of our collaborative culture and continued momentum.

April also marks a significant milestone with the addition of the Istanbul Airport (IST) THY Cargo Ramp Consolidation Area project to our portfolio — an important step forward in our business development efforts.

With the continued dedication and collaboration of our teams, we remain focused on delivering value and sustaining our growth in the period ahead.

New Joiners – April 2026

Necati Emre Kesici – Senior Alignment Engineer

Start Date: April 6, 2026

Necati Emre Kesici, a graduate of Civil Engineering from Middle East Technical University (2017), has gained significant experience in infrastructure and design engineering through his roles at Erbil Proje, Temelsu, Altaş, and AGM Mühendislik.

His hobbies include playing video games, table tennis, and swimming.

Önder Aygün – Senior Structural Engineer

Start Date: April 13, 2026

Önder Aygün, a graduate of Civil Engineering from Ege University (2006), has developed strong expertise in structural design through his experience at companies such as Kam Ankara Beton Sanayi A.Ş., Systra, and Kontrolmatik Technologies.

In his free time, he enjoys crossfit and web design.


We warmly welcome both of our colleagues and wish them a long and successful career journey as part of the ARX Türkiye family…

Highlights!
What a Project #2026-03 – From Metro Line to Data Center: Engineering in Türkiye

Our Deputy General Manager Ayşegül Gültekin Durgut and Group Manager Merve Özdemir Öğüt delivered their presentation on the global platform.

The IL03 Data Centre in Istanbul, a state-of-the-art digital infrastructure facility, offers approximately 10,000 m² of usable space with an IT capacity of up to 9 MW, featuring advanced electrical and cooling systems for high-performance operations.

The presentation also highlighted the Gayrettepe–Istanbul Grand Airport metro line, a major transportation project designed to enhance urban mobility. The 37 km fully underground line connects the city center directly to Istanbul Airport.

from What a Project #2026-03 Presentation
Welcoming Spring… 🌸

As ARX Türkiye's Istanbul & Ankara offices, we celebrated the arrival of spring with a delightful happy hour.

Taking a short break from our busy schedules and spending time together was truly refreshing.

Moments like these remind us of our strong team spirit and the value of creating together. We look forward to many more achievements and moments to celebrate together!

April, 2026
📸 Görsel yakında eklenecek
April, 2026
New Project! IST THY Cargo Ramp Consolidation Area

We have added the IST THY Cargo Ramp Consolidation Area Project to our portfolio, marking a significant step in strengthening our presence in large-scale aviation infrastructure projects.

The project is designed to enhance operational efficiency by minimizing aircraft turnaround time, ensuring seamless airside logistics to support the growing cargo capacity of SMARTIST, and optimizing Ground Support Equipment (GSE) traffic to maximize safety.

Photos of the Month 📸
Jordan Potash Railway Line Project
August 13, 2025 - Photo by Beytullah Demirci

We would like to thank Beytullah Demirci for sharing this photo.

The Wadi Rum section of the Jordan Phosphate Railway Line (Detailed Design) project is characterized by a typical arid desert landscape, marked by rugged rock formations and sparse vegetation. Sudden rainfall in such environments leads to short-term surface runoff, erosion, and sediment transport.

In this challenging geography, railway fieldworks carried out under extreme desert conditions reaching up to 45°C highlight both engineering discipline and operational resilience.

📸 Görsel yakında eklenecek
Announcements!
The project code for the Istanbul Turkish Airlines (THY) Cargo Ramp Consolidation Area Project has been defined as 87.1035.00001 THY Deincing.
ARX Türkiye's Ankara Office will enter a relocation process in June 2026 and move to its new office. We are excited about this new chapter and look forward to a smooth transition!
Drawing printing processes have been launched at our Istanbul office.

What's Next?

As we move forward, several key developments are on the horizon.

Our Ankara office will be relocating in June, opening a new chapter for ARX Türkiye. In parallel, we will be introducing a new, more user-friendly HR system designed to enhance efficiency and employee experience.

We also continue to focus on growth — both in terms of our team and new project opportunities.
